title: Bangladeshi student admitted to MIT EECS

ecs: Published research with BUET CSE faculty

whatWorked: Reverse-engineered MIT's preference for sustained technical depth: 3 years of competitive programming culminating in IOI medal + a published paper on graph algorithms. Demonstrated CS impact, not breadth.

title: Indian student admitted to Stanford CS

ecs: Built mental-health chatbot used by 12k users

whatWorked: Founder-mindset signal: shipped a real product with measurable users, not a hackathon prototype. Stanford essays linked the chatbot to a personal narrative about a family member's depression.

title: Pakistani student admitted to CMU SCS
whatWorked: Pure CS depth. CMU SCS rewards programming excellence + open-source impact over well-rounded profiles. Codeforces rating and LLVM PRs were the entire story.

title: Bangladeshi student admitted to Cambridge Maths
whatWorked: Cambridge Maths is brutal-subject-depth. STEP triple-1 + IMO medal + demonstrated mathematical maturity (undergrad-level books) made the interview a formality.

title: Indian student admitted to Oxford PPE
whatWorked: PPE rewards verbal & analytical reasoning. High TSA + sustained policy writing portfolio + debate trophies covered the three pillars of PPE.

title: Bangladeshi student admitted to Waterloo CS (co-op)
whatWorked: Waterloo's CS admit is contest- and work-experience-driven. Euclid 92 alone moved this applicant from waitlist to admit. AIF essays were tight and specific.

title: Bangladeshi student admitted to U of T Engineering Science
whatWorked: Engineering Science values curiosity across disciplines. Robotics + teaching + sustained academic excellence in math/physics fit the program's interdisciplinary brief. Strong supplemental application.

title: Indian student admitted to NUS CS (full scholarship)
whatWorked: NUS scholarship pool rewards strong testing + clear regional impact. Showcased measurable user numbers and India-context applicability.

title: Bangladeshi engineer awarded Fulbright (master's in Public Policy)

ecs: Led BRAC fintech pilot reaching 40k rural users

whatWorked: Fulbright weights leadership + return-impact. Concrete metrics (40k users) and a clear plan to return to Bangladesh's policy sector closed the application.

title: Bangladeshi student awarded Rhodes Scholarship

profile.school: Asian University for Women → Dhaka University
ecs: Founded NGO supporting Rohingya women's education (3 years, 1.2k beneficiaries)

whatWorked: Rhodes weights intellectual distinction + service leadership. Sustained NGO impact + published writing + clear post-Oxford return plan.

title: Bangladeshi student admitted to TUM Informatics (full)

profile.school: Notre Dame College, Dhaka → BUET 2 yr transfer

whatWorked: Low TUM tuition makes ROI strong. Motivation letter explicitly tied research interest to a TUM lab. German A2 signaled commitment.

title: Sri Lankan student admitted to Imperial Computing
whatWorked: Imperial filters on pure subject excellence. MAT score + IOI + portfolio of shipped projects covered the bar.

title: Bangladeshi engineer awarded Knight-Hennessy (Stanford MBA)

profile.school: BUET CSE → 5 yr at startup → Stanford GSB admit

whatWorked: Knight-Hennessy looks for civic-minded leaders. Founder of a healthtech with measurable impact + clear vision for return-to-region application.

title: Indian student awarded Gates Cambridge (PhD Computer Science)

profile.school: IIT Delhi → 1 yr research → Cambridge PhD admit

whatWorked: Gates Cambridge selects on academic excellence + commitment to improving lives. Research with concrete societal applications (medical imaging) made the case.

title: Bangladeshi student admitted to U of T on partial scholarship (financial constraint case)

ecs: Built personal-finance tracker used by 2k Bangladeshis

whatWorked: Demonstrated frugal impact in supplemental: built useful tools from limited resources. Need-aware schools (U of T, McMaster) valued the narrative.
